examples.text: مَتَى تَزُرْ قْوْمَ مَنْ تَهْوَى زِيَارَتَهَا / لَا يُتْحِفُوكَ بِغَيْرِ الْبِيضِ وَٱلْأَسَلِ

Abu Dhu'ayb Al-Hudhali
شَرِبْنَ بِمَاءِ ٱلْبَحْرِ ثُمَّ تَرَفَّعَتْ مَتَى لُجَجٍ خُضْرٍ لَهُنَّ نَئِيجُ
šaribna bimāʔi l-baḥri ṯumma taraffaʕat matā lujajin ḵuḍrin lahunna naʔīju
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

senses.gloss: Matthew, one of the Twelve Disciples of Jesus and one of the Four Evangelists
Matthew, the first of the four canonical gospels

etymology.text:
Compare مَا (mā, “what?”) and مَن (man, “who?”). Cognate to Hebrew מתי (matai).

From Hebrew מתי (mattai).
